### Post by Connollyir on 2006-02-16
This is not applicable to the other thread currently in this section - his only problem is resolution, I have other issues. I had a dual monitor setup in my previous install but now I can't seem to get it right (it took me 5 minutes last time...). This is my xorg.conf:

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/CID"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
        # paths to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/CID"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"GLcore"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

#Section "Device"
#	Identifier	"NVIDIA Corporation NVIDIA Default Card"
#	Driver		"vesa"
#	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
#EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"NVIDIA Corporation NVIDIA Default Card"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
    	Screen      0  
    	Option     "TwinView"
    	Option     "MetaModes"  "1280x1024,1280x1024"
    	Option     "TwinViewOrientation"      "RightOf"
    	Option     "SecondMonitorHorizSync"   "50-75"
    	Option     "SecondMonitorVertRefresh" "60-80"
	Option 	   "RenderAccel" 	      "true"
	Option 	   "AllowGLXWithComposite"    "true" 
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-51
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"NVIDIA Corporation NVIDIA Default Card"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

The commented out section is the original configuration (I like to do that until I get things working how I want them) and I use an Nvidia Geforce 7800GT. I have installed the Nvidia-GLX drivers and Nvidia settings through apt. My problem is that only one of the screens displays an image, the right one. The left one says it has no signal. The monitor works because they both show the post messages on boot and I was pretty sure this same configuration worked well last time. 

Can you see anything I should change to get them both working?

Thanks

-Ian

---

### Post by j2r7 on 2006-02-16
Hello Fellow N.H. Person, 
I'll offer more help, but I am going to Dapper and everything is "under construction" and all I can hear is broken glass everywhere. I think you need Display 2 setup , but like I said I check back after a reboot or tomorrow.  Here is my daul monitor config


# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# Test file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/CID"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
        # paths to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/CID"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"GLcore"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies, Inc. 3D Rage II+ 215GTB (Mach64 GTB)"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:0:13:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies, Rage 128 Pro Ultra"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"ULTRASCANP99 [1]"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"ULTRASCANP99 [2]"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Screen 1"
	Device		"ATI Technologies, Inc. 3D Rage II+ 215GTB (Mach64 GTB)"
	Monitor		"ULTRASCANP99 [1]"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "720x350" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "720x350" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "720x350" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "720x350" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "720x350" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "720x350" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Screen 2"
	Device		"ATI Technologies, Rage 128 Pro Ultra "
	Monitor		"ULTRASCANP99 [2]"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "720x350" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "720x350" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "720x350" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "720x350" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "720x350" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "720x350" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Simple Layout"
	Screen		"Screen 2"
	Screen		"Screen 1" Rightof "Screen 2"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard" "CoreKeyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option "Xinerama" "true"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection

Well heres the thing. I only have one screen in there because when I add a second one in there and call it something other than the name of the original screen gdm freaks out and dies on me. Its like I need some way to rename the "Default Screen"s so that I can add a new one and xorg will pick it up as my missing half. I have tried changing around the screen settings but nothing works.... it feels like I need to "scan my hardware" or something to tell xorg that I do have two monitors and they need different names. Is there a way to rename these things, because every time I do it in the xorg.conf file gdm dies. I'm convinced thats why my second display doesn't work.

Thanks

-Ian

### Post by ZylGadis on 2006-02-18
Here's my TwinView config (NVidia FX5200):

> # /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/CID"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
        # paths to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/CID"
EndSection

Section "Module"
#	Load	"GLcore"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
#	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"NVIDIA Corporation NV34 [GeForce FX 5200]"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Option		"NoLogo" "true"
	Option		"RenderAccel" "true"
	Option		"IgnoreDisplayDevices" "TV"
	Option		"NoRenderExtension" "false"
	Option		"Accel" "true"
	Option		"AllowGLXWithComposite" "true"
	Option		"ConnectedMonitor" "CRT,CRT"
	Option		"TwinView" "true"
	Option		"MetaModes" "1280x1024,1280x1024"
	Option		"SecondMonitorHorizSync" "28-64"
	Option		"SecondMonitorVertRefresh" "43-60"
	Option		"TwinViewOrientation" "LeftOf"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-64
	VertRefresh	43-60
	DisplaySize	675 270
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"NVIDIA Corporation NV34 [GeForce FX 5200]"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection


Mind the "ConnectedMonitor" option, I think that's your problem. I have two 17" LCD displays connected to the DVI (through a VGA-to-DVI adapter) and the VGA ports of the card. Since CRT (CRT here stands for VGA-connected, so never mind my two LCDs) monitors don't reply back when the card tries scanning them (or whatever that is, I dimly remember what I read when I set that up), you need to specify both. If you have a DVI-connected monitor all the way (i.e. without any adapters), you might skip that.
And, of course, I've no idea what you need for a TV.

Hope that helps :)

Edit: just noticed that another poster is actually telling you how to enable Xinerama. As you probably know, in your case there are two ways to enable the two monitors: one is NVidia native (TwinView - look at my config), the other is Xinerama (look at j2r7's config), which works with anything, as it is software (X) - based. Naturally, if you have a NVidia, you want the native way. Don't confuse them, I've heard bad things about the X of people who try enabling them simultaneously :)

### Post by Connollyir on 2006-02-23
[QUOTE=sdurst]Ian,

I've been down that road many times trying to get my dual-head configuration working again, but I've had no success.  Mind summarizing what your fix was?

Thanks,

-Steve[/QUOTE]

Sure. 

I borrowed some of the configuration from Alex.P (the options part under "' Identifier 'NVIDIA Corporation NV34 [GeForce FX 5200]'") to get a base of options to work with. 

I notived he used only one monitor section, which is how I did it last time but this time I seemed to be having issues with it. At any rate I created two separate entries for my monitors (like j2r7) in order to be on the safe side - keep in mind I'm not sure this is necissary, its just how it all worked out. 

I created a second "screen" section and labeled their Identifiers "Screen 1" and "Screen 2" - they had the same values so I simply copied and pasted (although the xorg I will post at the end of this might have parts of the screen 2 config cut off, it was the same as screen 1.... I don't know why kedit is showing it like that). 

Next I modified the "ServerLayout" section to reflect the additional screen and the new names I gave them. 

Thats it - restart xserver and you should see a nice Nvidia logo pop up across your screens. If you messed up you are going to get dropped off at the Unix terminal login (there are several built into every distro, switch between them using Ctr+Alt+Function key, your graphical interface xserver is on F7......... just in case you didn't know). If x doesn't display a set of error messages at that point, restart your window manager (kdm, gdm etc.) and then switch to the xorg terminal F7. Xserver will fail and give you a error output telling you where you messed up your conf file - use it to fix what went wrong and you should be all set from there. 

Its pretty simple, I just didn't have the time/was too lazy to sit down and figure it out  before now.

Good luck.

-Ian

---
